description.en: Play this if you want constant damage and sheer brutality
When you succeed on a melee attack, you can mark a Stress to roll an extra d6 and add it to the damage. You can do this a number of times per scene equal to your Proficiency.
Once per rest, when you would mark Stress, you can instead spend 1 Blood to ignore that Stress.
Once per scene when you defeat an adversary within Melee, gain a Hope and 1 Blood.

Once per long rest, when you succeed on a melee attack, you may expend any or all Blood from your Bloodforge to empower the strike. For each Blood spent, add +1d6 damage to the attack’s damage roll.

If this attack defeats its target, you may immediately make a melee attack against another adversary within Very Close range. That attack deals half the total extra damage you rolled from Blood.
Play this if you want control, counter-attacks, and shadowdriven disruption.

When you use a Midnight domain attack or feature against a foe within Close range, you can mark 1 Stress to force a Reaction Roll (13). On a failure, the target marks 1 Stress and is pulled one range step toward you. On a success, they’re temporarily Vulnerable until they mark a Hit Point. Once per scene.
Once per rest, when you take damage, you can vanish into shadow, instantly moving to any space within Close range and gaining advantage on your next melee attack.

Once per long rest, spend a Hope to emanate a blood-aura until the end of the scene. Adversaries within Very Close suffer a −1 penalty on Reaction Rolls. While your aura is active, each time you mark Stress, gain 1 Blood on your Bloodforge.

While your aura is active:

- Each time you mark a stress, gain 1 Blood in your Bloodforge.

- Your Bloodforge maximum increases by 3.
